setting.note: Dhaka in 1971, then the capital of East Pakistan, as the Bangladesh Liberation War begins. A widow and her grown children are drawn into the fight from a family house in the planned district of Dhanmondi, past the university where the March crackdown starts, out along the refugee road to Calcutta and a guerrilla camp near the border.

palette.note: The deep red of the liberation war and the flag that came out of it. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
